root_text.an6.91:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme abhabbo diṭṭhisampanno puggalo uppādetuṁ.
root_text.an6.91:1.3: Sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ, vicikicchaṁ, sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ rāgaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ dosaṁ, apāyagamanīyaṁ mohaṁ.
root_text.an6.91:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme abhabbo diṭṭhisampanno puggalo uppādetun”ti.

translation_text.an6.91:1.1: “Mendicants, an individual accomplished in view can’t give rise to six things.
translation_text.an6.91:1.3: Substantialist view, doubt, misapprehension of precepts and observances, and forms of greed, hate, and delusion that lead to rebirth in places of loss.
translation_text.an6.91:1.4: An individual accomplished in view can’t give rise to these six things.”
